title: The Beauty and Pain of Waiting
description: On pages 5–7, the beauty and pain of waiting as crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line.
scene: The Beauty and Pain of Waiting. Crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Why are you saying something like that? Who knows?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Maybe they are coming back.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's a beautiful thing to know that this whale is still keeping his promise.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But the trust is more cruel than the dream.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They are already gone! I have checked already.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They've already left from

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Grand line.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What did you say?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So, this whale was abandoned?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But if they are going to escape from here, they have to get through calm belt.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's right, so I don't know if they are still alive

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: or dead. Even if they are still alive,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They will never come back.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: No one wants to stay in this catastrophic atmosphere.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If you are not strong enough, you can't survive in this sea.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Because once you've experienced the fear of Grand Line, you will become weak.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: This means that they weren't strong enough and fear of death

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So they decided to leave this place without thinking of the promise.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: This whale has been ABAN- DONED EVEN

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Though he has believed in them for 50 years!!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: This is

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Too cruel!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Since you've already known the truth, why don't you tell him?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This whale can under- stand human Lang- usage, right?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I've already told him every- thing,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But Laboon

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: didn't listen.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Laboon. Listen to me.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Those pirates, who told me to take care of you,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: already left Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You are friends.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And the promise between you and them can't be a lie,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But they will never come back here.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the beauty and pain of waiting. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
